behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real git rename semantics plus CLI/report wiring. Rename detection depends on actual git diff/status behavior and thresholds; mocked git output can hide parser/flag mismatches that only show up with real subprocess output.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it (`git mv`), run the CLI against recent changes, and assert the report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk is often from real subprocess byte output/encoding and binary diff formatting. In-process fakes can miss decode, locale, and git-output edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI on the change set, assert process exit is successful and binary file count/category appears in the structured stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git state with specific exit codes/messages. This failure commonly appears only when invoking git on an actual repository state.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with no commits, run the CLI, and assert it exits cleanly and prints a valid empty structured report (not an exception or stderr-driven failure).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history and git’s own change classification over commits. Mocked history can pass while real git traversal/range handling fails.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temporary repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run the CLI for the target range, and assert each reported count matches the known ground truth for that real history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real index vs working-tree semantics and the git commands/flags used by the CLI. Mocks often flatten these states and can miss real discrepancies.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temporary repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios (same file edits), run the CLI in each analysis mode, and assert structured stdout is identical.
